release_provenance.meaning: This checkout is a deliberately curated, independently verifiable public output of a larger ongoing formal-mathematics workflow. No public claim depends on private or unreleased work.
release_provenance.boundary: The workflow provenance explains the release discipline; it does not imply hidden proof authority or equivalence with private work.

authority_posture.proof: Lean source checked by the pinned Lean kernel
authority_posture.navigation: generated projections and this descriptor; not proof authority
authority_posture.semantic_bridge: approximate correspondence only; never a proof edge
authority_posture.methodology: docs/methodology.json defines mathematical methodology and claim-transition requirements; it is not proof authority or claim-status authority

concept.source: scripts/query_corpus.py then attached claims or atlas

premise_chain.boundary: The public atlas does not claim elaborator-derived declaration dependencies or accessibility annotations.

status_taxonomy.proved here: Lean theorem in the committed formal-source checkpoint; no novelty assertion is implied
status_taxonomy.formalised here: Known mathematics represented and checked in Lean
status_taxonomy.unconditional progress: Genuine theorem that does not settle the open problem
status_taxonomy.conditional reduction: Exact implication whose remaining hypothesis is open
status_taxonomy.verified finite instance: Kernel-checked computation at a bounded range
status_taxonomy.cited only: External theorem used for positioning, not formalised

principal_claims.statement: For every integer b ≥ 2, the series ∑_{n≥1} 1/(bⁿ − 1) is irrational (Erdős, 1948).
E = ∑_{n≥1} 1/(2ⁿ − 1) is irrational, as the base-2 corollary of the full-support theorem.

For every base b ≥ 2, ∑_{n∈A} 1/(bⁿ − 1) is irrational for factorial support, power-of-two support, multiples, pairwise-coprime supports with summable reciprocals, eventually-periodic supports, residue classes, and the odd numbers. Proved families, not the universal statement.

S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ is not equal to p/q for any integer p and any 1 ≤ q ≤ 79 639 646 646 701 375 323 355 774 875 831 053 (≈ 7.96 × 10³⁴). A lower bound on any rational representation, not a proof of irrationality.

S is irrational if and only if certified non-integrality witnesses occur at unbounded parameters. The pointwise completeness theorem and the diagonal supply theorem together show that an unbounded lcm-diagonal supply is equivalent as well. Producing that supply remains open.

At fixed parameters, a certified kill exists exactly when the corresponding tail difference is non-integral. At every positive shift, integrality of one tail difference forces S to be rational; thus irrationality is equivalent to pointwise non-integrality, and to pointwise finite certificates.
Kernel-checked certified kills for small periods and at 28 explicit lcm-diagonal scales through t = 64, plus finite certificate shards, each discharged by decide.

At each lcm-diagonal scale, integrality is equivalent to a foreign-defect term landing in an explicit full-target interval. Globally, S is irrational if and only if the full target is avoided at arbitrarily large diagonal scales; that cofinal avoidance is not proved.

If H divides the reduced denominator of a rational x and the reduced denominator of c·x divides H for an integer c, then the complementary factor x.den/H divides |c|. Scalar localisation transfers, rather than discards, that denominator information; this local obstruction is not an irrationality criterion.

A rational support-series value forces every zero window in the divisor-count coefficient to have length at most ε·log₂(N+1) + O_{ε,c,v}(1), uniformly in the support. This constrains coverage; it does not solve universal #257.

The Mersenne achievement set is compact, perfect, totally disconnected, nowhere dense, and has Lebesgue measure one. Membership is equivalent to greedy survival at every level; for the target 1/2 it is exactly equivalent to the canonical greedy orbit omitting infinitely many exponents. No finite normalized support has value 1/2, so positive membership would automatically produce an infinite rational-valued support and refute universal #257. Finite exact-rational death certificates soundly prove non-membership (3/4 is excluded at level one). The strict-tail Cantor geometry is recorded by Kovač–Tao; no novelty claim is made for the formalised refinements.

non_claims.meaning: This release does not settle the universal Erdős #257 problem
This release does not settle Erdős #249
This release does not itself constitute a peer-reviewed publication record
This release is not asserted to be equivalent to any private development
Provider or model output is not proof authority; only the Lean kernel check is
No unpublished proof sketch or hidden artefact carries proof authority for this release

remaining_open_propositions.statement: Prove that S = ∑ φ(n)/2ⁿ is irrational, with no bound on a possible rational denominator.
Produce certified non-integrality witnesses at unbounded parameters. A sufficient one-parameter form is to produce them along the lcm diagonal.
Decide whether 1/2 lies in the Mersenne achievement set. Yes refutes universal #257; no closes only this route.
Prove irrationality for every infinite support A, not only the named support families formalised here.

mathematical_programmes.title: Achievement-set geometry and the half-value seam
Boolean Möbius carry and global rationality constraints
Transport, curvature, phase separation, and no-go results
Coprimality probability, gcd moments, and Stern–Brocot geometry
Half-carry compactness, windows, seams, and finite propagation
Denominator, CRT, moment, and gauge obstructions

edge_semantics.corollary_of: The source claim is presented as a corollary or specialization of the target claim.
edge_semantics.builds_on: The source claim's argument layer builds on the target claim or criterion.
edge_semantics.eliminates_case: The source claim eliminates a named branch or cell in the target reduction without discharging its remaining residual.
edge_semantics.makes_exact: The source claim records why a reduction or certificate formulation loses no information.
edge_semantics.instantiates: The source claim supplies checked finite instances of the target reduction.
edge_semantics.advances_open_target: The source claim is honest partial progress toward the open target and does not settle it.
edge_semantics.re_expresses_open_branch: The source claim gives an exact reformulation of a distinct open branch without deciding that branch or the surrounding open target.
edge_semantics.positions: The source claim is cited or used to position another claim; it is not formal proof authority here.

remaining_open_effect.statement: Proves the claim for named infinite-support families while leaving arbitrary infinite supports open.
For finite-core orthogonal-petal bouquets, reduces irrationality at base 2 to the stated uniform tail-selection predicate; that selector is not proved.
Identifies half-membership exactly with cofinal terminal omissions and unbounded skipped ranks, but proves neither side of that equivalence.
Reduces the positive half-membership branch to future-tail escape at the remaining middle transitions; that escape statement is not proved.

Restricts a hypothetical final middle -2 cell to 412 of 2730 joint residue classes after the exact rank-26 seam prefix; the surviving classes, the middle -1 cell, and the right-tail branch remain open.
Excludes every rational representation up to the stated denominator bound without proving irrationality.
Re-expresses the open irrationality step as the unbounded supply of checked finite certificates.
Re-expresses diagonal non-integrality as avoidance of an exact full-target interval; an unbounded avoidance supply remains open.
Transfers the open supply obligation to exact fresh-loss projections and binary suffix gaps without asserting that such a supply exists.
Re-expresses the canonical strict-jump centrality supply as the sign of one exact integer slack; cofinal nonnegative slack remains open.
Places the finite foreign-residue projection behind an explicit analytic-complement bridge and an unbounded separated-projection supply; neither is asserted.
Adds a necessary reciprocal-mass condition for a rational value over an infinite support.
Adds a necessary sublogarithmic zero-window condition for a rational value over an infinite support.
Provides exact global geometry and finite non-membership tests for the value set without proving the universal irrationality statement.

Localizes an unsafe isolated greedy skip to an exact open interval of width below 1/9; it neither proves that the actual half-greedy orbit avoids the interval nor addresses arbitrary infinite supports.
Gives exact block-safety, tail-gap, and primitive-overshoot witnesses sufficient for half-membership, but proves no cofinal supply of those witnesses and does not address arbitrary infinite supports.
Reduces membership of the target one half to an exact rational separation statement from level seven onward; it neither proves that statement nor settles arbitrary infinite supports.
Re-expresses diagonal non-integrality through several exact transport and curvature observables while leaving every unbounded supply open.
Turns a cofinal first-harmonic estimate into the existing finite certificate supply; no such estimate is proved.
Gives an exact four-term first-harmonic decomposition and a sufficient 9X/10 budget; no prime-distribution or decorrelation estimate supplies those budgets cofinally.
Re-expresses the missing supply as cofinal adjacent-pair phase separation; no such supply is proved.
Re-expresses the missing supply as cofinal pivot anti-reconstruction or window-pair separation; no cofinal arithmetic producer is proved.

human_capsule: The exact boundary between proved and open must remain explicit.
Lean checks the stated proposition; it does not check that the proposition expresses the intended mathematics.
A public claim is transferred between representations only through a proved implication or equivalence.
A reduction must state the hypothesis or quantifier that remains open.
A finite result remains finite unless a theorem supplies the unbounded or universal step.
Progress toward an open problem must state which outstanding obligation it proves, narrows, decomposes, re-expresses, or constrains.

change_classes.summary: A proof body changes; the stated proposition does not
change_classes.public_consequence: No claim or exposition change follows automatically. Compare the axiom audit before and after.

change_classes.summary: A stated proposition or its hypotheses change
change_classes.public_consequence: Reconcile the claim wording, the remaining-open boundary, and the paper statement.

change_classes.summary: The transitive assumptions of a declaration change
change_classes.public_consequence: Reassess the proof posture even when the proposition is unchanged.

change_classes.summary: A claim moves between coordinates, encodings, or models
change_classes.public_consequence: Record the transport theorem, its direction, and anything not preserved.

change_classes.summary: A checked bounded range or certificate family is extended
change_classes.public_consequence: Keep the finite status; update only the stated bound.

change_classes.summary: A claim's declaration coordinates or links are corrected; the claim is otherwise unchanged
change_classes.public_consequence: Link or correct the supporting declarations of an unchanged claim, then regenerate dependent projections. The mathematical claim does not change; a Lean check confirms the linked declaration exists and elaborates.

change_classes.summary: An advances-open-target relation is added or changed
change_classes.public_consequence: Record the outstanding obligation affected and the exact relation.

change_classes.summary: The status of a public claim changes
change_classes.public_consequence: State the theorem or review that licenses the new status.

change_classes.summary: Authored paper or documentation prose changes mathematically
change_classes.public_consequence: Prose must not exceed the status recorded in the claim registry.
Regenerate below the existing owners; no mathematical effect.
Regenerate METHODOLOGY.md and the corpus descriptor. Rules route review; they do not decide mathematics.

migration_from_v2.reason: Version 2 embedded exhaustive graphs and full declaration prose in a surface described as compact.

field_replacements.compact_graph.claims: compact_graph.principal_claims; expand through docs/claims.json or query_corpus.py
